# ContextBoundary — Full LLM Digest

Version: v0.1
License: Apache 2.0
Repo: https://github.com/kannanokannan/ContextBoundary

---

## What is ContextBoundary?

ContextBoundary is a vendor-neutral technical-layer reference framework.
It defines how AI systems should handle data boundaries across vendors,
jurisdictions, and privacy tiers.

It is NOT a runtime system. It is a specification layer.
The runtime enforcement is handled by ContextOps.

---

## The Three-Layer Model

Layer 3 — Client Solution Overlay (Client-owned)
  Components: Business rules, Configuration scope, Output preferences

Layer 2 — Domain Knowledge (Framework-owned — the moat)
  Components: Agent logic, Knowledge library, Evaluation framework

Layer 1 — Vendor LLM (Vendor-owned — substitutable)
  Components: LLM client adapter, Model & API version, Cost / tokens

ContextBoundary is the contract at the Layer 1 / Layer 2 boundary.
It defines what the vendor can touch, what data tier crosses that line,
and what compliance obligations apply.

---

## Five-Zone Boundary Model

Z1 — Public inference: no PII, open context
Z2 — Authenticated inference: session-bound, user-identified
Z3 — Enriched context: structured personal data in prompt
Z4 — Regulated data: compliance-scoped, audit-required
Z5 — Restricted / sovereign: jurisdiction-locked, air-gapped

---

## Privacy Tier Classification

Tier I   — Low risk: anonymised, aggregated, public data
Tier II  — Medium risk: pseudonymised, session-scoped data
Tier III — High risk: identified, regulated, cross-border data

---

## Jurisdictional Audit Profiles (v0.1)

GDPR (gdpr.md)
  - Lawful basis required for all processing
  - Data subject rights: access, erasure, portability, objection
  - Controller / processor distinction enforced at zone boundary
  - Retention limits mapped per zone
  - Cross-border transfer restrictions apply at Z4 and Z5

Planned for v0.2: DPDP, RBI, HIPAA, SOC2, EU AI Act

---

## Relationship to ContextOps

ContextBoundary  = specification layer (what is allowed, classified, audited)
ContextOps       = execution layer (how agents behave within those boundaries)

ContextBoundary does not enforce. It defines.
ContextOps reads ContextBoundary profiles and enforces them at runtime.

---
